settings.title: Two-story rear addition
settings.summary: Six hundred square feet added to a foursquare — a family room below, a primary suite above, and a roofline that looks like it was always there.
settings.description:
The family loved their street and their neighbors but had outgrown the house. Moving meant leaving both; adding meant getting the details right so the addition didn't announce itself.

We matched the original siding profile, sourced windows with the same sightlines as the 1920s originals, and carried the roof pitch through so the rear elevation reads as one building. Inside, a new family room opens to the garden, and the primary suite upstairs finally gave the parents a door that closes.

Framed, roofed, and weathertight before the fall rains; finished in fourteen weeks total. The neighbors' verdict — that they couldn't tell where the old house ends — is the one we build for.
